rows.label: statement
rows.left: He **should have called** the doctor earlier.

rows.label: negative
rows.left: She **shouldn't have left** without saying goodbye.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Remember that some verbs have irregular past participle forms (e.g. go → gone, take → taken, write → written).

content.subtitle: Use
items: To say someone had the chance or the ability to do something in the past but did not — **could have**: I **could have won** the race, but I tripped near the finish line.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Use **could + bare infinitive** for a general past ability: When I was ten, I **could swim** quite well.
Use **could have + past participle** for an opportunity that existed in the past but was not taken: I **could have swum** in the competition, but I chose not to.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To criticise a past action or say what the right thing to do would have been — **should have / ought to have**: You **ought to have checked** the instructions before you started.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Strong probability — **must have / can't have**: She was laughing so much — it **must have been** a really funny film. / He **can't have eaten** — the food is still untouched.
Possibility — **could have / may have / might have**: They left very early, so they **might have taken** the wrong train.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To express that something was expected to happen in the past but did not — **should have / ought to have**: The delivery **should have arrived** by yesterday — I ordered it a week ago.

sections.title: A: Complete with modal perfect forms
sections.instructions: Use the correct modal perfect form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: We had enough time, so we ...... (finish) the project yesterday.
Mia looks exhausted. She ...... (sleep) very well last night.
You ...... (tell) me about the meeting. I waited for an hour!
They arrived very early, so they ...... (leave) home before six.
I ...... (buy) the tickets online, but I forgot.
Sam ...... (be) at school today because he's on holiday.
You ...... (not / spend) all your money on games.
Leo ...... (take) your umbrella by mistake. He was in a hurry.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Circle the correct modal phrase in each sentence.

items.sentence: I **could have / should have** gone to the dentist, but I didn't.
You **must have / might have** been very happy when you passed the exam!
They **can't have / shouldn't have** known about the surprise party.
You **should have / may have** called me before you came.
Amy **might have / ought to have** forgotten our appointment.
We **could have / must have** won, but we missed two easy goals.
Dad **must have / should have** left his phone at work. It's not here.
I **ought to have / can't have** been more careful with that vase.

sections.title: C: Criticism and expectation
sections.instructions: Complete with should have, shouldn't have or ought to have.

items.sentence: You ...... studied harder for the test.
We ...... left so late; now we're stuck in traffic.
I ...... listened to your advice.
They ...... sent that rude message.
She ...... arrived by now. I'm getting worried.
You ...... told your parents the truth.
He ...... spent all his savings on that car.
The bus ...... come ten minutes ago.

sections.title: D: Probability and possibility
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with must have, can't have, may have or might have.

items.sentence: The ground is wet. It ...... rained during the night.
Julia isn't answering. She ...... gone to bed already.
Ben got 100% in the test. He ...... revised a lot.
That noise ...... come from the kitchen. It's locked.
They ...... missed the train, so they'll be late.
This letter ...... arrived today because the post has already come.
Sophie looks relaxed now. She ...... finished her homework.
The keys aren't here. I ...... left them in the car.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with a modal perfect. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: You should told me earlier.
They must had forgotten the time.
I could have went with you.
She can't have saw him at school.
We ought have to leave earlier.
He shouldn't have took my bike.
It may have be a misunderstanding.
You might have forget your password.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I'm sure Carla forgot about the meeting. **MUST**
Carla ...... about the meeting.

It wasn't a good idea to shout at your brother. **SHOULDN'T**
You ...... at your brother.

Perhaps Dan left his jacket on the bus. **MIGHT**
Dan ...... his jacket on the bus.

I had the chance to call you, but I didn't. **COULD**
I ...... you, but I didn't.

It is impossible that Ella wrote this email. **CAN'T**
Ella ...... this email.

It was wrong not to apologise. **OUGHT**
You ...... apologise.
